Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.

Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.

SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.
- obsidian-markdown: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.

file.content:
Function Get-LapsPassword {
    <#
    .SYNOPSIS
        Retrieves the Microsoft Entra LAPS password for a specified computer via Microsoft Graph.

    .DESCRIPTION
        Connects to Microsoft Graph using provided credentials (client secret or interactive)
        and retrieves the LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) password for the
        specified device name. Requires the DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All and Device.Read.All
        Graph permissions.

    .PARAMETER ComputerName
        The display name of the device as registered in Entra ID / Azure AD.

    .PARAMETER Username
        The username for interactive authentication. If omitted, interactive login will be used.

    .PARAMETER Password
        The password for interactive authentication. If omitted, interactive login will be used.

    
    .EXAMPLE
        # Interactive authentication
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123"
    
    .EXAMPLE
        # Using credentials
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -Username "user@contoso.onmicrosoft.com" -Password "password"
        
    .NOTES
        Author:  Chuck Littlefield
        Date:    2026-04-16

        Prerequisites:
            Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser -Repository PSGallery -Force

        Required Graph permissions (delegated or application):
            Device.Read.All
    #>

    [CmdletBinding()]
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory=$true, HelpMessage = "Display name of the device in Entra ID")]
        [string]$ComputerName,

        [Parameter(Mandatory=$False,HelpMessage = "Username")]
        [string]$Username,

        [Parameter(Mandatory=$False, HelpMessage = "Password")]
        [string]$Password
    )


    # Verify Microsoft.Graph module is available
    if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name Microsoft.Graph)) {
        throw "Microsoft.Graph module is not installed. Run: Install-Module Microsoft.Graph -Scope CurrentUser -Repository PSGallery -Force"
    }

    try {
        # ── Connect to Microsoft Graph ──────────────────────────────────────────
        If ($Username -and $Password) {
            $cred = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential($Username, (ConvertTo-SecureString $Password -AsPlainText -Force))
            Write-Verbose "Connecting to Microsoft Graph interactively..."
            Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Device.Read.All" `
                            -NoWelcome `
                            -Credential $cred `
                            -ErrorAction Stop
        }
        else {
            Write-Verbose "Connecting to Microsoft Graph interactively (delegated)..."
            Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "Device.Read.All", "DeviceLocalCredential.Read.All" -NoWelcome -ErrorAction Stop
        }

        Write-Verbose "Connected to Microsoft Graph."

       

        # ── Retrieve the LAPS credential ───────────────────────────────────────
       
        $lapsCredential = Get-LapsAADPassword -Device $computerName -includepassword 



        return $lapsCredential
    }

    catch {
        Write-Error "Error retrieving LAPS password: $_"
    }
    finally {
        Disconnect-MgGraph -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
        Write-Verbose "Disconnected from Microsoft Graph."
    }
}

content.text:
Could you share the actual error message you're seeing? That will help pinpoint the issue.

That said, I can already spot one likely problem: `Connect-MgGraph` does **not** support a `-Credential` parameter for delegated (username/password) authentication. The Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK doesn't accept `PSCredential` for interactive user flows — it's only valid for certain app-only (service principal) flows.

For delegated user authentication with a username/password (ROPC flow), you need to use `Connect-MgGraph` with a pre-acquired token, or use the `-TenantId` + `-ClientId` parameters with the `ClientCredentials` grant.

Paste your error and I'll give you the exact fix.

Error retrieving LAPS password: Parameter set cannot be resolved using the specified named parameters. One or more parameters issued cannot be used together or an insufficient number of parameters were provided.

content.thinking:
The error "Parameter set cannot be resolved using the specified named parameters" confirms that `-Credential` is not a valid parameter for `Connect-MgGraph` in the way it's being used. 

`Connect-MgGraph` doesn't support a `-Credential` parameter for username/password (ROPC) flows. The valid parameter sets for `Connect-MgGraph` are:
- Interactive: `-Scopes`
- App-only with client secret: `-TenantId`, `-ClientId`, `-ClientSecretCredential`
- App-only with certificate: `-TenantId`, `-ClientId`, `-CertificateThumbprint` or `-CertificateName`


- Access token: `-AccessToken`

For delegated user authentication with username/password, I'd need to obtain an access token first using MSAL or the `Get-MsalToken` cmdlet, then pass it to `Connect-MgGraph` via the `-AccessToken` parameter. The `-Credential` parameter was removed in newer versions of the Microsoft.Graph module, so the best approach is either switching to interactive authentication or using the MSAL.PS module for the ROPC flow.

For this script's purpose of retrieving LAPS passwords, the simplest solution is to remove the credential parameters entirely and rely on interactive authentication, or implement ROPC through MSAL.PS to acquire a token manually. Since this is for internal IT use, I could keep the username/password parameters but clarify that they don't work with delegated flows, or remove them entirely to simplify the implementation. I'm settling on a cleaner approach: remove the broken credential parameters entirely and offer two working paths instead—interactive authentication for manual use, or app-only authentication with a service principal for automation scenarios. For LAPS retrieval specifically, the app-only approach with the right device permissions would be the most reliable for enterprise automation.

content.text:
Confirmed. `Connect-MgGraph` does not support `-Credential` (PSCredential/ROPC flow) in the current SDK. The username/password path is broken by design.

The right approach for automation is a **service principal** (app-only) using `-TenantId`, `-ClientId`, and `-ClientSecretCredential`. I'll replace the broken Username/Password parameters with those.

input.old_string:
.PARAMETER Username
        The username for interactive authentication. If omitted, interactive login will be used.

    .PARAMETER Password
        The password for interactive authentication. If omitted, interactive login will be used.

    
    .EXAMPLE
        # Interactive authentication
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123"
    
    .EXAMPLE
        # Using credentials
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -Username "user@contoso.onmicrosoft.com" -Password "password"
input.new_string:
.PARAMETER TenantId
        Azure AD tenant ID (GUID or domain). Required for app-only authentication.

    .PARAMETER ClientId
        App registration client ID. Required for app-only authentication.

    .PARAMETER ClientSecret
        Client secret for the app registration. Required for app-only authentication.

    .EXAMPLE
        # Interactive authentication
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123"

    .EXAMPLE
        # App-only (service principal) authentication
        Get-LapsPassword -ComputerName "DESKTOP-ABC123" -TenantId "contoso.onmicrosoft.com" -ClientId "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx" -ClientSecret "your-secret"
